seo.title: MedLens+ — Turn lab reports into visit-ready timelines
seo.description: Upload scattered lab PDFs and see what changed over time. MedLens+ builds timelines from your reports so you're ready for your next appointment.

hero.headline: Your lab history, finally in one timeline.
hero.subheadline: Upload PDFs from any lab. MedLens+ pulls out the numbers, tracks what moved since your last visit, and helps you walk in with better questions.
hero.privacyNote: We'll only email you about early access. No spam, no selling your data.

socialProof.label: CBC, LFT, KFT, Lipid, HbA1c, Thyroid panels
Built for tracking results over months and years
Uses the reference ranges on your actual reports
Not a substitute for professional medical advice

problem.title: Every visit adds another PDF to the pile

items.title: No single timeline
items.description: Each test lands as a separate file. Comparing LDL from March to November means opening three folders, not glancing at one chart.

items.title: Ranges vary by lab
items.description: The interval on your Quest report isn't the same as the one on a hospital printout. Generic web ranges can make a normal result look alarming.

items.title: Visits feel rushed
items.description: You want to ask about that HbA1c bump, but digging through email attachments the night before takes longer than the appointment itself.

productPreview.title: See what changed, not just today's numbers
productPreview.description: MedLens+ extracts values from your uploads, plots them on a timeline, and highlights shifts since your last test — always using the ranges printed on your documents.

items.title: Upload reports
items.description: Drop in PDFs or photos from any lab. We extract structured values and keep your originals secure.

items.title: Compare over time
items.description: View markers on a timeline, spot trends, and get a heads-up when you're comparing results from different labs.

items.title: Prep for your visit
items.description: Export a focused brief with the changes that matter and questions worth asking — all tied back to your uploaded reports.

items.title: Multi-report timeline
items.description: Track the same markers across months and years in one longitudinal view.

items.title: Your report's ranges
items.description: Flags use the reference intervals on your uploads, not generic tables from the internet.

items.title: Missing-data detection
items.description: Know when a panel looks incomplete so you can ask your clinician about gaps.

items.title: Visit brief
items.description: Export a summary and suggested questions before your next appointment.

items.title: Cross-lab caution
items.description: Clear warnings when you're comparing results from different laboratories or methods.

items.title: Built-in boundaries
items.description: We organize your reports. We don't tell you what disease you have or what to take.

comparison.title: Why not just a folder of PDFs?

items.title: Diabetes monitoring
items.description: Track HbA1c, glucose, and related panels across recurring visits.

items.title: Thyroid tracking
items.description: Follow TSH and related markers as doses or circumstances change.

items.title: Caregivers & family
items.description: Help a parent or partner organize reports before specialist visits.

trust.title: How we handle health information
trust.description: MedLens+ explains what your reports show. We don't tell you what condition you have, what medication to take, or whether you need treatment.
items: Never diagnoses disease or prescribes medication
Always uses reference ranges from your uploaded reports
Warns when comparing results from different labs
Encourages discussion with qualified healthcare professionals

items.question: What does MedLens+ actually do?
items.answer: It turns scattered lab PDFs into a timeline you can review before appointments. You'll see how values changed, which ranges applied on each report, and a short list of topics worth raising with your clinician.

items.question: Which report types work in beta?
items.answer: We're starting with common panels: CBC, liver and kidney function, lipid profile, HbA1c, thyroid, and key vitamins. More formats roll out as waitlist feedback comes in.

items.question: Is early access free?
items.answer: Yes. The public beta is free for waitlist members — no credit card required. See our pricing page for what's included.

items.question: Where can I read more?
items.answer: The full FAQ covers privacy, safety, and data handling in detail. Our blog and glossary have practical guides on organizing reports and reading reference ranges.

finalCta.title: Join the public beta
finalCta.description: Be among the first to turn years of lab PDFs into a timeline built for real clinical conversations.
